highlights.text: I warned about this on Friday at 4pm, stating: “After a move like this, ES often needs to digest the move…My general lean if I had to give one though is that ES can cooloff now and do some backtesting and work on structure….Shorter term, 6407 and 6398 are key supports next week.” This is what we spent Monday and Tuesday doing exactly, working on a range mostly ~6400 to 6430s.
I wrote at 4pm Tuesday “My general lean is always defer to the trend.
My lean is any traps under are bought, then we head up to 6415, 6428, 6436”.
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “My general lean is to defer to the trend.

My lean is work this range more, and when ready to break we see 6450, 6457.” This played out, and we tagged 6457 by midnight last night overshot by about 10 points, then from there, retraced the entire rally from yesterday, selling to a new low by 2:20PM.
Two of those building blocks are 1) My core setup, the Failed Breakdown.
As readers know, the core feature of FOMC days are traps/Failed Breakdowns.
If we are drifting into it, one can buy it, but if not, just wait for the Failed Breakdown of today’s low.” We flushed yesterdays low, trapped shorts, and up we went.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into some quality Failed Breakdowns we had on Monday/Tuesday, and I’ll also discuss the setup that caused yesterday’s FOMC squeeze (it was a hybrid Back-Test/Level Reclaim setup, rare).
6404-07 to 6438=a new consolidation range, with 6415 magnet in the middle.

post_title: The FOMC Day Squeeze Gets Retraced In SPX. Will The Dip Get Bought? August 1st Plan
